content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 31, 1990


Ross G. Hubbard, Town Manager
2100 Donald Drive
P.O. Box 188
Moraga, CA  94556

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. I-90-700

Dear Mr. Hubbard:

     This is in response to your November 21, 1990, written
request for advice concerning the mass mailing provisions of the
Political Reform Act ("Act").1  Because your letter lacks some
facts (discussed below) necessary for us to fully answer your
question, we provide you with only informal advice.  (Regulation
18329, copy enclosed.)

QUESTION

     May the Town of Moraga list the names of its councilmembers
in its recreation brochure and what you refer to as "other similar
publications," along with the names of the park and recreation
commissioner and staff members?

CONCLUSION

     We lack sufficient facts to say with certainty that the
publications at issue fall within the definition of mass mailing.
If we assume they do, the town would be permitted to include in
them the names of its councilmembers, along with those of the park
and recreation commissioner and staff members -- provided that (1)
the councilmembers' names appear in a roster listing, (2) the
listing contains the names of all elected officers of the town,
and (3) their names are printed in the same type size, typeface,
type color and location.

_______________________
1    Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Our File No. I-90-700
Page 2


FACTS

The Town of Moraga routinely mails two publications to 6,000
households within the town. One is a newsletter; the other a
recreation brochure. They are prepared by the town staff without
"direct input" from the town council.

It appears from your letter that the town also publishes, or
is considering publishing, other publications similar to the
newsletter, recreation brochure, or both.

ANALYSIS

Section 89001 says, "No newsletter or other mass mailing
shall be sent at public expense." Subdivision (a) of Regulation
18901 (copy enclosed) defines "mass mailing" for purposes of
Section 89001. Under that subdivision, an item will be considered
a mass mailing if all of these criteria are met:

(1) [The] item sent is delivered, by any
means, to the recipient at his or her
residence, place of employment or business, or
post office box....

(2) The item sent either:

(A) Features an elected officer
affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing, or

(B) Includes the name, office,
photograph, or other reference to an elected
officer affiliated with the agency which
produces or sends the mailing, and is prepared
or sent in cooperation, consultation, co-
ordination, or concert with the elected
officer.

(3)(A) Any of the costs of distribution
[are] paid for with public moneys; or

(B) Costs of design, production, and
printing exceeding $50.00 are paid with public
moneys, and the design, production, or
printing is done with the intent of sending
the item other than as permitted by this
regulation.

Our File No. I-90-700
Page 3


     (4)  More than two hundred substantially
similar items are sent, in a single calendar
month....

     In this case, we know that more than 200 copies of the same
recreation brochure are mailed to residences at public expense.
If such brochures include the names or other references to the
councilmember, the criteria for a mass mailing have been met.2

     However, even if we assume that all these criteria have been
met and that the recreation brochure and other similar
publications therefore constitute mass mailings, your proposal to
include the names of the councilmembers would still be permissible
if certain requirements, set forth in Subdivision (b)(1) of
Regulation 18901, are met.  In your situation, the applicable
requirements are that (1) the councilmembers' names appear in a
roster listing, (2) the listing contains the names of all elected
officers of the town, and (3) their names are printed in the same
type size, typeface, type color and location.

     I hope this responds to your inquiry.  If you have any other
questions, feel free to call me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel


                                        _______________
                                        By:  Steven R. Rhoads
                                        Counsel Legal Division

SH:SR:dg

Enclosures




2     You say in your letter that the brochures have been prepared
without "direct input" from members of the town council.  If the
brochure is produced completely independent of the councilmembers,
names of councilmembers may be included in the brochure so long as
they are not "featured."  (Regulation 18901(a)(2)(A) and (c)(2);
Smookler Advice Letter A-89-374, Welihan Advice Letter I-89-63,
and Bulens Advice Letter A-89-215, copies enclosed.)
